You are auditing source code for an INDEPENDENT external code review (client: Telus). Tools: Bash, Read.

STEP 0 — SKIP IF DONE. Run: test -s /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_6.tsv && echo DONE || echo TODO
If it prints DONE, this shard is already complete — reply exactly "shard 6: cached" and STOP. Do nothing else.

STEP 1 — get your file list: /opt/conda/bin/python -c "import json; print(chr(10).join(json.load(open('/home/polaris/wt/outline_agent/.name_shards.json'))[6]))"
It prints lines "relpath:START-END" relative to /home/polaris/wt/outline_agent.

STEP 2 — GENUINELY READ every assigned file/range line by line with the Read tool (offset/limit from START-END; use limit up to 2000 per call to minimize calls). Do NOT grep-and-skim — read the code.

STEP 3 — flag EVERY name (file/module, class, func/method, var, const, enum value) that looks UNPROFESSIONAL in an external review:
  - marketing/quality adjectives: honest, real, true, smart, magic, ultimate, enhanced, improved, robust, aggressive, killer, awesome, blazing, super, mega, best
  - temporal/version markers: v2/v3/v4, r2/r3, _new, _old, legacy, deprecated, next/nextgen, final(meaning 'this version'), fixed, temp, wip, draft, backup, orig, working, experimental, proto
  - jokes/informal/vague/slang: junk, garbage, hack, spy, stub(as a real name), yolo, stuff, misc, foo/bar, doit, thing, blah, landmine, nuke, magic-number-as-word, terse cryptic abbreviations
Judge at a STRICT bar: **when in doubt, mark RENAME.** Only KEEP-BUT-NOTED for unambiguously standard, descriptive, professional vocabulary or a REAL domain term genuinely implemented (finalize, proper_noun, evict_older_than, SmartArt only if it truly makes SmartArt).

STEP 4 — APPEND TAB-separated rows to /tmp/claude-1000/-home-polaris-polaris-project/21e87760-8436-4090-870d-99ef2121882e/scratchpad/name_findings/shard_6.tsv (one per flagged name), using printf with real tabs:
  VERDICT<TAB>NAME<TAB>relpath:LINE<TAB>KIND<TAB>WHY<TAB>SUGGESTED_NAME
  VERDICT=RENAME or KEEP-NOTED; KIND=file/class/func/var/const/enum. If a file is fully clean, write: NONE<TAB>-<TAB>relpath<TAB>-<TAB>clean<TAB>-

Reply ONLY: "shard 6: <R> renames, <K> kept across <F> files".

attachment.content:
- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

1	"""Query-gen BAKE-OFF: all 10 methods vs the coverage scoreboard (I-qgen-001, GH #1291).
2	
3	Executes the locked brief: FLOOR (current POLARIS) + the 9 runnable frontier methods (WebWeaver,
4	IterResearch, ConvergeWriter, WARP, DuMate, FS-Researcher, ScaffoldAgent, PokeeResearch, DOLORES)
5	— DeepResearch-R1/9K is trained-only, noted-not-faked. Each method generates its queries against
6	the SHARED retrieve() under an EQUAL query budget, and is scored on COVERAGE = DRB-II info_recall
7	(finding-level), the operative metric. Highest coverage wins. No end-to-end report, no rendering.
8	
9	Every method uses the SAME reasoning-excluded GLM-5.2 (query-gen + judge) and the SAME retrieve()
10	(cache for the floor's deterministic queries, live fetch for the frontier methods' novel queries),
11	so only the query LOGIC differs. Blocked DRB-II source dropped before scoring (no cheating).
12	"""
13	
14	from __future__ import annotations
15	
16	import argparse
17	import json
18	import os
19	import sys
20	from concurrent.futures import ThreadPoolExecutor, as_completed
21	
22	from scripts.dr_benchmark.gate0_lineage import SLUG_TO_IDX
23	from scripts.dr_benchmark.qgen_coverage_harness import (
24	    CoverageBudget,
25	    load_blocked_references,
26	    load_canonical_question,
27	    load_required_points,
28	    make_blocked_filter,
29	)
30	from scripts.dr_benchmark.qgen_methods import FloorMethod
31	from scripts.dr_benchmark.qgen_frontier_methods import build_frontier_methods
32	from scripts.dr_benchmark.qgen_metrics import breadth_recall, build_digest, finding_coverage, make_cache_only_retrieve
33	from scripts.dr_benchmark.run_qgen_coverage import floor_queries, make_glm_judge_llm
34	from scripts.dr_benchmark.run_qgen_determination import floor_budget
35	
36	
37	def run_task(slug: str, judge_llm, allow_fetch: bool, judge_workers: int) -> dict:
38	    idx = SLUG_TO_IDX[slug]
39	    question = load_canonical_question(idx)
40	    points = load_required_points(idx)
41	    blocked = load_blocked_references(idx)
42	    is_blocked = make_blocked_filter(blocked)
43	    budget = CoverageBudget(max_queries=floor_budget(slug, question),
44	                            max_query_rounds=int(os.getenv("PG_QGEN_MAX_QUERY_ROUNDS", "3")))
45	    raw_retrieve = make_cache_only_retrieve(domain=None, allow_fetch=allow_fetch)
46	
47	    def retrieve(query):
48	        return [r for r in raw_retrieve(query) if not is_blocked({"url": r.get("url"), "text": r.get("text")})]
49	
50	    # FLOOR + the 9 frontier methods, all on the reasoning-excluded GLM-5.2.
51	    methods = [FloorMethod(facets=floor_queries(slug, question), name="floor")] + build_frontier_methods(judge_llm)
52	    method_workers = int(os.getenv("PG_QGEN_METHOD_WORKERS", "3"))
53	
54	    def score_method(m) -> dict:
55	        try:
56	            corpus = m.generate_corpus(question, retrieve, budget)
57	            br = breadth_recall(corpus)
58	            digest = build_digest(corpus)
59	            fc = finding_coverage(points, digest, judge_llm, workers=judge_workers)
60	            row = {"method": m.name, "finding_coverage": fc.coverage, "finding_covered": fc.covered,
61	                   "finding_total": fc.total, "distinct_sources": br.distinct_sources,
62	                   "distinct_findings": br.distinct_findings, "n_queries": getattr(m, "_n_queries", None)}
63	        except Exception as exc:  # fail loud per method, keep the rest
64	            row = {"method": m.name, "error": repr(exc), "finding_coverage": -1.0}
65	        fc_v = row.get("finding_coverage")
66	        print(f"[bakeoff] {slug} {m.name:<15} coverage={fc_v:.3f} "
67	              f"sources={row.get('distinct_sources','?')} findings={row.get('distinct_findings','?')} "
68	              f"q={row.get('n_queries','?')}", flush=True)
69	        return row
70	
71	    # Methods are independent -> run them concurrently (bounded, to avoid a fetch storm).
72	    results = []
73	    with ThreadPoolExecutor(max_workers=method_workers) as ex:
74	        futs = {ex.submit(score_method, m): m.name for m in methods}
75	        for fut in as_completed(futs):
76	            results.append(fut.result())
77	
78	    results.sort(key=lambda r: r.get("finding_coverage", -1), reverse=True)
79	    return {"slug": slug, "idx": idx, "required_points": len(points),
80	            "equal_budget_queries": budget.max_queries, "ranking": results}
81	
82	
83	def main(argv=None) -> int:
84	    p = argparse.ArgumentParser(description="Query-gen bake-off: 10 methods vs DRB-II info_recall coverage")
85	    p.add_argument("--slugs", nargs="*", default=["drb_72_ai_labor"])
86	    p.add_argument("--allow-fetch", action="store_true", default=True)
87	    p.add_argument("--no-fetch", dest="allow_fetch", action="store_false")
88	    p.add_argument("--judge-workers", type=int, default=8)
89	    p.add_argument("--out", default="outputs/qgen_coverage/bakeoff.json")
90	    args = p.parse_args(argv)
91	
92	    if os.getenv("PG_QGEN_AUTHORIZED_SPEND") != "1":
93	        print("[bakeoff] needs PG_QGEN_AUTHORIZED_SPEND=1 (the bake-off spends). Aborting.", file=sys.stderr)
94	        return 3
95	
96	    judge_llm = make_glm_judge_llm()  # reasoning-excluded GLM-5.2: query-gen + coverage judge
97	    tasks = []
98	    for slug in args.slugs:
99	        try:
100	            tasks.append(run_task(slug, judge_llm, args.allow_fetch, args.judge_workers))
101	        except Exception as exc:
102	            print(f"[bakeoff] TASK FAILED {slug}: {exc!r}", file=sys.stderr)
103	
104	    # Overall ranking: mean coverage per method across tasks.
105	    by_method: dict[str, list[float]] = {}
106	    for t in tasks:
107	        for r in t["ranking"]:
108	            if r.get("finding_coverage", -1) >= 0:
109	                by_method.setdefault(r["method"], []).append(r["finding_coverage"])
110	    overall = sorted(((m, sum(v) / len(v)) for m, v in by_method.items()), key=lambda x: x[1], reverse=True)
111	
112	    report = {"slugs": args.slugs, "tasks": tasks,
113	              "overall_mean_coverage": [{"method": m, "mean_coverage": c} for m, c in overall],
114	              "winner": overall[0][0] if overall else None}
115	    os.makedirs(os.path.dirname(args.out), exist_ok=True)
116	    with open(args.out, "w", encoding="utf-8") as h:
117	        json.dump(report, h, ensure_ascii=False, indent=2)
118	
119	    print("\n=== BAKE-OFF: query-gen methods ranked by DRB-II info_recall coverage ===")
120	    for rank, (m, c) in enumerate(overall, 1):
121	        print(f"  #{rank:<2} {m:<15} mean_coverage={c:.3f}")
122	    print(f"  WINNER: {report['winner']}")
123	    print(f"[bakeoff] wrote {args.out}")
124	    return 0
125	
126	
127	if __name__ == "__main__":
128	    raise SystemExit(main())
129

1	"""Gate-B production caller — wires the native 4-role evaluation into the honest sweep.
2	
3	I-meta-002 PR-9/M3b. This is WIRING ONLY: it constructs the verifier `RoleTransport` for the
4	three verifier roles (Mirror / Sentinel / Judge — the generator stays on OpenRouter, upstream
5	of this transport), sets `PG_FOUR_ROLE_MODE`, builds a no-argument CLOSURE over the native
6	input builder (`build_native_gate_b_inputs`) + the deterministic evidence normalization, and
7	hands the transport + builder into `run_one_query`.
8	
9	TRANSPORT ROUTING (I-meta-007d, operator directive 2026-06-01): the verifier transport is
10	ENV-GATED via `PG_FOUR_ROLE_TRANSPORT`:
11	  - "openrouter" (DEFAULT at THIS dev/benchmark stage): all 3 verifier roles route via
12	    OpenRouter with MAX reasoning (`OpenRouterRoleTransport`). This avoids standing up the
13	    self-hosted vLLM GPU fleet just to run the benchmark — it saves the Vast.ai GPU rental
14	    cost. SOVEREIGNTY: OpenRouter is a US router; this is acceptable for dev/benchmark ONLY,
15	    NOT for the sovereign demo (feedback_sovereignty_threat_model). The lock is NOT mutated —
16	    it still pins `serving_route: vast_self_host*`, which remains the FINAL SOVEREIGN
17	    destination; only the runtime routing is overridden, and only at this stage.
18	  - "self_host": the original `OpenAICompatibleRoleTransport` POSTing per-role
19	    `PG_<ROLE>_BASE_URL` self-hosted vLLM endpoints — the lock's sovereign serving route.
20	
21	CONTAMINATION-CRITICAL (§-1.1, operator-locked): every input is built ONLY from NATIVE
22	config — the scope template's `per_query_report_contract[<slug>].required_entities` and the
23	D8 release-policy config. This module NEVER reads anything under `outputs/dr_benchmark/`
24	(gold rubric / freeze pin / competitor answers).
25	
26	NO SPEND / NO NETWORK at import. The transport's `httpx.Client` is created INSIDE
27	`build_gate_b_transport` (not at module level), so importing this module never opens a client
28	or touches a socket. The Gate-B LIVE run (real endpoints + real spend) is the later
29	operator-authorized canary; this module is exercised offline by the seam test with a FAKE
30	transport injected in place of `build_gate_b_transport`'s output.
31	"""
32	
33	from __future__ import annotations
34	
35	# I-wire-014 (#1336): native-thread-safety clamp MUST run before ANY native import (torch/MKL/
36	# tokenizers read their thread-pool env vars once, at library-init time). Importing this standalone
37	# stdlib-only module IS the clamp (setdefault; LAW VI). Fixes the intermittent A15 re-fetch crash
38	# ``malloc(): unsorted double linked list corrupted`` — the MinerU VLM Predict racing on the glibc
39	# heap with leaked AccessBypass fetch daemon threads. Output-invariant (thread count != numerics).
40	# Codex gate P1: bootstrap the repo root onto sys.path FIRST so ``src.*`` imports whether this module
41	# is imported (run_gate_b_query) OR executed by path from repo root (sys.path[0]=scripts/dr_benchmark).
42	# This file is scripts/dr_benchmark/run_gate_b.py => repo root is parents[2] (NOT parents[1]).
43	import sys as _sys_clamp_bootstrap
44	from pathlib import Path as _Path_clamp_bootstrap
45	
46	_sys_clamp_bootstrap.path.insert(0, str(_Path_clamp_bootstrap(__file__).resolve().parents[2]))
47	import src._polaris_native_thread_safety  # noqa: F401,E402  # import-time side effect: applies the clamp
48	
49	import json
50	import logging
51	import os
52	import re
53	from dataclasses import dataclass
54	from pathlib import Path
55	from typing import Any, Callable, Mapping
56	
57	import httpx
58	
59	# I-ready-018 (#1138, Codex iter-1 P1-1): install the import-root alias BEFORE any repo import. The
60	# beat-both run launches from repo-root WITHOUT PYTHONPATH=src, so src/sitecustomize.py does NOT
61	# auto-load here. The core run path is src.-consistent (unaffected), but the --upload-file path imports
62	# polaris_v6 internals via the BARE root, which would ModuleNotFoundError root-only. Installing the
63	# alias here makes any bare polaris_graph/polaris_v6 import resolve to its src. counterpart. Idempotent
64	# + a no-op when the alias is already installed (sitecustomize) or the canonical tree is absent.
65	from src._polaris_import_alias import install_import_root_alias
66	
67	install_import_root_alias()
68	
69	
70	def enable_faulthandler() -> None:
71	    """GH #1260: dump a C+Python stack on the NEXT native crash instead of dying
72	    silently. A libxml2 SIGSEGV in any worker thread kills the process with no
73	    traceback; `faulthandler` installs a signal handler that prints the faulting
74	    stack (all threads) so the next crash is diagnosable rather than a mystery.
75	
76	    Honors `PYTHONFAULTHANDLER` (already enabled by the interpreter then) and is
77	    idempotent — calling `faulthandler.enable` twice is safe."""
78	    import faulthandler
79	    import signal
80	
81	    try:
82	        faulthandler.enable(all_threads=True)
83	    except (RuntimeError, ValueError, OSError):
84	        # stderr redirected to a non-fileno stream (e.g. captured under a test
85	        # harness) — best-effort; never block the run on diagnostics setup.
86	        pass
87	
88	    # I-deepfix-001 (wedge diagnosis): register SIGUSR1 to dump EVERY thread's
89	    # Python stack to stderr ON DEMAND without killing the process. `kill -USR1
90	    # <pid>` then captures an in-process DEADLOCK (e.g. the shared-SSL-context
91	    # lock/import hang) that `faulthandler.enable` — which only fires on a FATAL
92	    # signal — can never surface. POSIX-only (SIGUSR1 absent on Windows).
93	    try:
94	        if hasattr(signal, "SIGUSR1"):
95	            faulthandler.register(signal.SIGUSR1, all_threads=True, chain=False)
96	    except (RuntimeError, ValueError, OSError, AttributeError):
97	        pass
98	
99	from scripts.architecture.verify_lock import load_lock
100	# I-ready-017 FIX-JO (#1100): the canonical journal_only runtime-flag NAME. Imported (not
101	# re-hardcoded) so the per-slug activation below sets the SAME env the consumer reads in
102	# run_honest_sweep_r3.run_one_query -> journal_only_active (single source of truth, LAW VI).
103	# journal_only_filter imports only stdlib (os/dataclasses/urllib) — NO network/spend at import.
104	from src.polaris_graph.nodes.journal_only_filter import JOURNAL_ONLY_FLAG
105	from src.polaris_graph.roles.native_gate_b_inputs import (
106	    NativeGateBBundle,
107	    build_native_gate_b_inputs,
108	    normalize_evidence_pool_lookup,
109	)
110	from src.polaris_graph.roles.openai_compatible_transport import (
111	    OpenAICompatibleRoleTransport,
112	)
113	from src.polaris_graph.roles.openrouter_role_transport import (
114	    FOUR_ROLE_STAGE,
115	    OpenRouterRoleTransport,
116	    _family_from_slug,
117	    benchmark_verifier_family,
118	    benchmark_verifier_lineup,
119	    openrouter_base_url,
120	    role_reasoning_enabled,
121	)
122	# I-deepfix-001 Codex gate P0 (WS-1 judge cache run-scope): the judge verdict idempotency cache is a
123	# WITHIN-ONE-REPORT byte-twin dedup surface, but it is process-wide and (pre-fix) never reset — so a
124	# document-1 verdict leaked into document-2 in the same sequential sweep process. reset_judge_verdict_cache
125	# is called at the per-document boundary (top of run_gate_b_query) so each report's 4-role pass starts
126	# clean. judge_adapter imports only stdlib + the roles contracts at module top (no network/spend).
127	from src.polaris_graph.roles.judge_adapter import reset_judge_verdict_cache
128	from src.polaris_graph.roles.release_policy import load_d8_policy_config
129	
130	# The three verifier roles this caller serves (the generator is excluded — it runs live on
131	# OpenRouter, upstream of the per-claim verifier transport).
132	_VERIFIER_ROLES = ("mirror", "sentinel", "judge")
133	
134	# Env flag the guarded sweep branch reads to activate the 4-role seam.
135	_FOUR_ROLE_MODE_ENV = "PG_FOUR_ROLE_MODE"
136	
137	# I-meta-007d: which verifier transport to build (LAW VI env-gate). DEFAULT "openrouter" at the
138	# CURRENT dev/benchmark stage (saves GPU cost). "self_host" selects the lock's sovereign route.
139	_FOUR_ROLE_TRANSPORT_ENV = "PG_FOUR_ROLE_TRANSPORT"
140	_TRANSPORT_OPENROUTER = "openrouter"
141	_TRANSPORT_SELF_HOST = "self_host"
142	_FOUR_ROLE_TRANSPORT_DEFAULT = _TRANSPORT_OPENROUTER
143	
144	# OpenRouter catalog endpoint (GET) for the benchmark-stage slug-resolution preflight.
145	_OPENROUTER_MODELS_PATH = "/models"
146	
147	# httpx client timeout knob (LAW VI). I-meta-008 FULL-POWER: use the generous verifier timeout
148	# (default 900s) so the client default never undercuts the reasoning verifiers' per-request budget.
149	_TIMEOUT_SECONDS = int(os.getenv("PG_VERIFIER_LLM_TIMEOUT_SECONDS", "900"))
150	
151	
152	def four_role_transport_mode() -> str:
153	    """Resolve `PG_FOUR_ROLE_TRANSPORT` (I-meta-007d). DEFAULT "openrouter" at the dev/benchmark
154	    stage (saves GPU cost; OpenRouter is a US router — dev/benchmark ONLY, never the sovereign
155	    demo). "self_host" selects the lock's `vast_self_host*` route. Fails loud on an unknown value
156	    so a typo never silently picks the wrong (spend) route.
157	    """
158	    mode = os.getenv(_FOUR_ROLE_TRANSPORT_ENV, _FOUR_ROLE_TRANSPORT_DEFAULT).strip().lower()
159	    if mode not in (_TRANSPORT_OPENROUTER, _TRANSPORT_SELF_HOST):
160	        raise ValueError(
161	            f"{_FOUR_ROLE_TRANSPORT_ENV}={mode!r} is invalid; expected "
162	            f"{_TRANSPORT_OPENROUTER!r} (benchmark-stage OpenRouter, default) or "
163	            f"{_TRANSPORT_SELF_HOST!r} (lock sovereign self-host route)."
164	        )
165	    return mode
166	
167	
168	def build_gate_b_transport(
169	    http_client: httpx.Client | None = None,
170	) -> OpenRouterRoleTransport | OpenAICompatibleRoleTransport:
171	    """Construct the verifier transport (one shared sync `httpx.Client`), ENV-GATED per
172	    `PG_FOUR_ROLE_TRANSPORT` (I-meta-007d).
173	
174	    Built INSIDE this function (never at module level) so importing `run_gate_b` opens no
175	    client and touches no socket.
176	
177	    - "openrouter" (DEFAULT, dev/benchmark stage — saves GPU cost): returns an
178	      `OpenRouterRoleTransport` that, when invoked, POSTs `/chat/completions` to OpenRouter with
179	      each role's lock-pinned slug AND MAX reasoning. SOVEREIGNTY: OpenRouter is a US router —
180	      acceptable for dev/benchmark ONLY; the lock's `vast_self_host*` self-host route remains
181	      the FINAL SOVEREIGN destination (the lock is NOT mutated).
182	    - "self_host": returns the original `OpenAICompatibleRoleTransport` (per-role
183	      `PG_<ROLE>_BASE_URL` self-hosted vLLM) — the lock's sovereign serving route.
184	
185	    `http_client` (P2): the live caller passes nothing, so a real `httpx.Client` is built here;
186	    tests inject a `MockTransport`-backed client so transport-selection is exercised with no live
187	    client and no socket. Resolved ONCE and handed to whichever transport is selected.
188	
189	    The live invocation is the later operator-authorized Gate-B canary, never a test.
190	    """
191	    client = http_client if http_client is not None else httpx.Client(timeout=_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)
192	    if four_role_transport_mode() == _TRANSPORT_OPENROUTER:
193	        return OpenRouterRoleTransport(client)
194	    return OpenAICompatibleRoleTransport(client)

197	def verifier_model_slugs() -> dict[str, str]:
198	    """Return the pinned `{mirror,sentinel,judge: model_slug}` map for the ACTIVE transport mode.
199	
200	    MODE-AWARE (I-meta-007d P1-1) so served == pinned on whichever route is active:
201	      - "openrouter" (benchmark, DEFAULT): the BENCHMARK lineup
202	        (`benchmark_verifier_lineup` — the operator-chosen OpenRouter slugs in
203	        role_selection_final.md: Mirror `z-ai/glm-5.1`, Sentinel `ibm-granite/granite-4.1-8b`,
204	        Judge `qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b`). The transport POSTs these exact slugs as `body["model"]`,
205	        so the pin the builder threads into `RoleRequest.model_slug` matches the served slug.
206	      - "self_host": the lock's self-host `model_slug`s (Mirror `cohere/command-a-plus`,
207	        Sentinel the granite-GUARDIAN, Judge `qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b`) — the sovereign serving
208	        route, served by `OpenAICompatibleRoleTransport` against `PG_<ROLE>_BASE_URL`.
209	
210	    The single machine-readable source of truth per mode (LAW VI). The generator slug is
211	    intentionally excluded — it is not a per-claim verifier role.
212	    """
213	    if four_role_transport_mode() == _TRANSPORT_OPENROUTER:
214	        return benchmark_verifier_lineup()
215	    lock = load_lock()
216	    return {role: lock["required_roles"][role]["model_slug"] for role in _VERIFIER_ROLES}

219	def make_gate_b_input_builder(
220	    *,
221	    d8_config_path: str | Path | None = None,
222	) -> Callable[..., NativeGateBBundle]:
223	    """Return the Gate-B builder CLOSURE — a factory over RESOLUTION POLICY only.
224	
225	    The closure captures ONLY the resolution policy (the optional `d8_config_path` + the
226	    normalization fn + the lock-slug source) — NOT the run-local report objects, which do not
227	    exist when the caller constructs the builder (they are produced inside `run_one_query` after
228	    generation). The SEAM calls the closure AFTER generation, passing the run-local objects
229	    (`multi`, `template`, `slug`, `domain`, `ev_pool`) as keyword args. The closure then OWNS
230	    resolution: it normalizes the run's raw `ev_pool` into the builder's
231	    `{evidence_id: {text, doi?, pmid?, url?}}` record contract (deterministic, no network — keys
232	    preserve the ProvenanceToken.evidence_id space), loads the D8 policy config, sources the
233	    pinned verifier slugs from the lock, and calls the native `build_native_gate_b_inputs`.
234	    NATIVE-ONLY: nothing here reads the gold rubric.
235	    """
236	
237	    def _builder(
238	        *,
239	        multi: Any,
240	        template: dict,
241	        slug: str,
242	        domain: str,
243	        ev_pool: Mapping[str, Mapping[str, Any]],
244	    ) -> NativeGateBBundle:
245	        evidence_lookup = normalize_evidence_pool_lookup(ev_pool)
246	        d8_config = load_d8_policy_config(d8_config_path)
247	        model_slugs = verifier_model_slugs()
248	        return build_native_gate_b_inputs(
249	            multi=multi,
250	            template=template,
251	            slug=slug,
252	            domain=domain,
253	            evidence_lookup=evidence_lookup,
254	            model_slugs=model_slugs,
255	            d8_config=d8_config,
256	        )
257	
258	    return _builder

270	def assert_four_role_families_distinct() -> dict[str, str]:
271	    """Assert the 4 roles (generator + the 3 verifiers) are all DISTINCT lineages for the
272	    ACTIVE transport mode (the N-way two-family invariant, CLAUDE.md §9.1 / family_policy:
273	    all_distinct). Fails LOUD (I-meta-007) — a collision would let one family self-verify.
274	    Returns the `{role: family}` map for telemetry.
275	
276	    MODE-AWARE (I-meta-007d P1-1): the family check must run over the families ACTUALLY served:
277	      - "openrouter" (benchmark, DEFAULT): generator family from the lock (`deepseek` — the
278	        generator is unchanged + runs upstream on OpenRouter) UNION the 3 BENCHMARK verifier
279	        families (`benchmark_verifier_family`: Mirror `z-ai`, Sentinel `ibm-granite`, Judge
280	        `qwen`). These four — deepseek / z-ai / ibm-granite / qwen — are distinct. (Checking the
281	        LOCK's verifier families here would assert on `cohere`/`ibm-granite`/`qwen`, which are NOT
282	        the families served on this route.)
283	      - "self_host": the lock's families for all 4 roles (the sovereign route).
284	    """
285	    lock = load_lock()
286	    if four_role_transport_mode() == _TRANSPORT_OPENROUTER:
287	        # I-beatboth-008 (#1285) P1-1: derive the generator family the SAME provider-prefix way
288	        # the verifiers do (`benchmark_verifier_family` -> `_family_from_slug` = slug.split("/")[0]),
289	        # from the generator's ACTIVE slug (PG_GENERATOR_MODEL override, else the lock's model_slug).
290	        # The prior code read the lock's `family` FIELD ('glm') for the generator while the verifiers
291	        # report the provider PREFIX ('z-ai' for z-ai/glm-5.2) — two inconsistent label spaces, so on
292	        # the all-GLM-5.2 stack gen='glm' vs mirror='z-ai' never matched by string and the
293	        # distinctness loop passed by ACCIDENT (it never consulted allowed_collisions). Deriving the
294	        # generator family the identical provider-prefix way makes gen='z-ai' == mirror='z-ai' -> the
295	        # real same-lineage collision is DETECTED -> the allowed_collisions [[generator, mirror]] pair
296	        # below is what permits it (emptying allowed_collisions now correctly RAISES).
297	        generator_slug = os.getenv("PG_GENERATOR_MODEL") or str(
298	            lock["required_roles"]["generator"]["model_slug"]
299	        )
300	        fams = {"generator": _family_from_slug(generator_slug)}
301	        for role in _VERIFIER_ROLES:
302	            fams[role] = benchmark_verifier_family(role)
303	    else:
304	        roles = ("generator", *_VERIFIER_ROLES)
305	        fams = {r: str(lock["required_roles"][r]["family"]) for r in roles}
306	    # I-beatboth-008 (#1285): honor the lock's family_policy.allowed_collisions instead of a
307	    # bare set-length distinctness check. On the all-GLM-5.2 stack the openrouter branch passes
308	    # only by LABEL MISMATCH — the generator's family is sourced from the lock ('glm') while the
309	    # mirror's benchmark slug 'z-ai/glm-5.2' yields the provider-prefix family 'z-ai' — i.e. the
310	    # same GLM-5.2 lineage carries two labels, so the old len(set)!=len() check passed by accident.
311	    # The self_host branch sources ALL four families from the lock, so gen+mirror are BOTH 'glm':
312	    # a real same-family collision that must be skipped ONLY for the operator-approved
313	    # allowed_collisions pair. A NON-listed same-family collision (e.g. a re-pick that puts the
314	    # Judge into the Mirror's family) still RAISES. Pattern mirrors validate_role_families
315	    # (openrouter_client.py:774-787): continue-without-recording so a THIRD same-family role raises.
316	    fp = lock.get("family_policy", {})
317	    allowed = {tuple(sorted(str(x) for x in pair)) for pair in fp.get("allowed_collisions", [])}
318	    seen = {}
319	    for role, fam in fams.items():
320	        if fam in seen:
321	            other_role = seen[fam]
322	            if tuple(sorted([role, other_role])) in allowed:
323	                continue
324	            raise RuntimeError(
325	                "Gate-B preflight: 4-role family collision — all roles must be distinct "
326	                f"lineages for the active transport mode (got {fams}); role {role!r} and "
327	                f"role {other_role!r} share family {fam!r} and the pair is not in "
328	                f"allowed_collisions={sorted(allowed)}"
329	            )
330	        seen[fam] = role
331	    return fams
332

344	    endpoints: dict[str, str] = {}

351	def _fetch_openrouter_catalog(http_client: httpx.Client | None = None) -> list[dict]:
352	    """Fetch the OpenRouter model catalog (`GET {base}/models`) and return its `data` list.
353	
354	    The `http_client` is INJECTABLE so the preflight test feeds a faked catalog via
355	    `httpx.MockTransport` (no real network, no spend). When None, a real sync client is created
356	    against `OPENROUTER_BASE_URL` (LAW VI) — that path only runs on a live, operator-authorized
357	    benchmark run. Fails loud (LAW II) on a non-200 status or a body without a `data` list.
358	    """
359	    url = f"{openrouter_base_url()}{_OPENROUTER_MODELS_PATH}"
360	    own_client = http_client is None
361	    client = http_client or httpx.Client(timeout=_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)
362	    # I-deepfix-001 (#1344): the pre-spend catalog probe must ride out a TRANSIENT OpenRouter
363	    # hiccup (HTTP 408/425/429/5xx or a connect/read timeout) instead of crashing the whole run to
364	    # a $0 abort on a single blip (observed: a lone 408 killed the run before retrieval started).
365	    # Bounded retry with capped exponential backoff; a GENUINE persistent failure STILL fails LOUD
366	    # (LAW II). Faithfulness-neutral (pre-spend liveness probe only, no spend, no gate touched).
367	    # PG_PREFLIGHT_CATALOG_RETRIES (default 5); the injected MockTransport test path returns 200 on
368	    # the first attempt so it is byte-identical.
369	    import time  # local import — module has no top-level `time` (mirrors the local imports elsewhere)
370	    try:
371	        _catalog_retries = max(1, int(os.getenv("PG_PREFLIGHT_CATALOG_RETRIES", "5")))
372	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
373	        _catalog_retries = 5
374	    _transient_status = {408, 425, 429, 500, 502, 503, 504}
375	    try:
376	        for _attempt in range(_catalog_retries):
377	            try:
378	                response = client.get(url, timeout=_TIMEOUT_SECONDS)
379	            except httpx.HTTPError as _exc:  # connect / read / pool timeout — transient transport fault
380	                if _attempt < _catalog_retries - 1:
381	                    time.sleep(min(2.0 * (2 ** _attempt), 30.0))
382	                    continue
383	                raise RuntimeError(
384	                    f"OpenRouter catalog preflight: GET {url} transport error after "
385	                    f"{_catalog_retries} attempts: {_exc}"
386	                ) from _exc
387	            if response.status_code == httpx.codes.OK:
388	                break
389	            if response.status_code in _transient_status and _attempt < _catalog_retries - 1:
390	                time.sleep(min(2.0 * (2 ** _attempt), 30.0))
391	                continue
392	            raise RuntimeError(
393	                f"OpenRouter catalog preflight: GET {url} returned HTTP {response.status_code}"
394	                + (f" after {_attempt + 1} attempts" if _attempt else "")
395	            )
396	    finally:
397	        if own_client:
398	            client.close()
399	    body = response.json()
400	    data = body.get("data")
401	    if not isinstance(data, list):
402	        raise RuntimeError(
403	            f"OpenRouter catalog preflight: GET {url} body has no `data` list (got "
404	            f"{type(data).__name__})"
405	        )
406	    return data
407	
408	
409	def preflight_openrouter_roles(http_client: httpx.Client | None = None) -> dict[str, str]:
410	    """LIVE-run preflight (openrouter mode, I-meta-007d): resolve each verifier role's BENCHMARK
411	    lineup slug against OpenRouter's catalog so a non-resolving slug fails BEFORE the sweep spends
412	    a token, AND assert the 4 families are distinct.
413	
414	    Resolves each role's BENCHMARK slug (`benchmark_verifier_lineup` — the operator-chosen
415	    OpenRouter slugs in role_selection_final.md: Mirror `z-ai/glm-5.1`, Sentinel
416	    `ibm-granite/granite-4.1-8b`, Judge `qwen/qwen3.6-35b-a3b`; P1-1) — NOT the lock's self-host
417	    slugs (Cohere's Mirror + the granite-GUARDIAN Sentinel are not on OpenRouter). The same lineup
418	    the transport POSTs, so a slug that resolves here is the slug that gets served. Each is
419	    asserted present in the OpenRouter catalog as either an entry `id` OR its `canonical_slug`
420	    (OpenRouter exposes both; a slug may match either). Returns the `{role: benchmark_slug}` map.
421	    The `http_client` is INJECTABLE so the test feeds a faked catalog (no network). ONLY called
422	    when building the real transport (skipped when a fake transport is injected for offline tests).
423	
424	    Fails LOUD (LAW II) on a missing slug or a family collision — never a silent skip.
425	    """
426	    assert_four_role_families_distinct()
427	    catalog = _fetch_openrouter_catalog(http_client)
428	    # Map EVERY catalog alias (entry `id` AND `canonical_slug`) -> that entry's
429	    # `supported_parameters` list. `setdefault` so the first occurrence of an alias wins (a later
430	    # duplicate id never clobbers it). A missing or non-list `supported_parameters` becomes `[]`,
431	    # which the executability check below reads as "advertises no reasoning".
432	    slug_params: dict[str, list] = {}
433	    for entry in catalog:
434	        if not isinstance(entry, dict):
435	            continue
436	        params = entry.get("supported_parameters")
437	        if not isinstance(params, list):
438	            params = []
439	        for key in ("id", "canonical_slug"):
440	            value = entry.get(key)
441	            if value:
442	                slug_params.setdefault(value, params)
443	
444	    resolved: dict[str, str] = {}
445	    lineup = benchmark_verifier_lineup()
446	    for role in _VERIFIER_ROLES:
447	        slug = lineup[role]
448	        if slug not in slug_params:
449	            raise RuntimeError(
450	                f"OpenRouter catalog preflight: role {role!r} benchmark slug {slug!r} is NOT in "
451	                f"the OpenRouter catalog (neither as an entry `id` nor `canonical_slug`). The "
452	                "benchmark-stage OpenRouter route cannot serve it — fix the benchmark lineup "
453	                f"(PG_{role.upper()}_MODEL / role_selection_final.md) or select "
454	                f"{_FOUR_ROLE_TRANSPORT_ENV}={_TRANSPORT_SELF_HOST}."
455	            )
456	        # Executability check (Codex iter-2 P1): a reasoning-enabled role sends MAX reasoning with
457	        # `provider.require_parameters=True`. If its benchmark slug does NOT advertise `reasoning`
458	        # in OpenRouter `supported_parameters`, OpenRouter refuses to route (no provider honors the
459	        # param) and the FIRST call fails. Catch it here, before any token is spent.
460	        if role_reasoning_enabled(role) and "reasoning" not in slug_params[slug]:
461	            raise RuntimeError(
462	                f"OpenRouter catalog preflight: role {role!r} is reasoning-enabled (sends MAX "
463	                f"reasoning + provider.require_parameters=True), but its benchmark slug {slug!r} "
464	                f"does NOT advertise `reasoning` in OpenRouter `supported_parameters` (advertised: "
465	                f"{slug_params[slug]!r}); with require_parameters=True OpenRouter would fail routing "
466	                f"at the first {role!r} call. Fix the benchmark lineup (PG_{role.upper()}_MODEL) to "
467	                f"a reasoning-capable slug, disable reasoning for this role "
468	                f"(PG_{role.upper()}_REASONING=0), or select "
469	                f"{_FOUR_ROLE_TRANSPORT_ENV}={_TRANSPORT_SELF_HOST}."
470	            )
471	        resolved[role] = slug
472	    return resolved
473

475	def preflight_four_role_transport() -> dict[str, str]:
476	    """LIVE-run preflight dispatcher (I-meta-007d): run the preflight matching the ENV-gated
477	    transport mode. openrouter -> resolve slugs against the OpenRouter catalog; self_host ->
478	    resolve each `PG_<ROLE>_BASE_URL`. Both assert the 4-distinct-family invariant. Returns the
479	    mode's `{role: resolved}` map.
480	    """
481	    if four_role_transport_mode() == _TRANSPORT_OPENROUTER:
482	        return preflight_openrouter_roles()
483	    return preflight_self_host_roles()

487	# diff-gate iter-1). A future gate/manifest reader checks `stage` to tell a benchmark OpenRouter
488	# run apart from the sovereign self-host serving path.
489	FOUR_ROLE_STAGE_MARKER_FILENAME = "four_role_stage.json"

492	def four_role_stage_marker() -> dict[str, object]:
493	    """Build the machine-readable stage marker for the ACTIVE transport mode (P2).
494	
495	    For the OpenRouter benchmark route the `stage` is `FOUR_ROLE_STAGE` ("benchmark_openrouter")
496	    and the marker records the active verifier lineup slugs/families so a future gate can never
497	    mistake an OpenRouter benchmark run for the sovereign self-host serving path (the lock's
498	    `serving_route: vast_self_host*`). For the self_host route the `stage` is "sovereign_self_host".
499	    """
500	    mode = four_role_transport_mode()

502	        return {
503	            "stage": FOUR_ROLE_STAGE,
504	            "transport_mode": mode,
505	            "verifier_lineup": benchmark_verifier_lineup(),
506	            "verifier_families": {r: benchmark_verifier_family(r) for r in _VERIFIER_ROLES},
507	            "note": (
508	                "benchmark-stage OpenRouter route (US router; dev/benchmark ONLY). The lock's "
509	                "self-host slugs (cohere Mirror, granite-GUARDIAN Sentinel) remain the SOVEREIGN "
510	                "destination — see role_selection_final.md + openrouter_role_transport docstring."
511	            ),
512	        }
513	    return {
514	        "stage": "sovereign_self_host",
515	        "transport_mode": mode,
516	        "verifier_lineup": verifier_model_slugs(),
517	    }

520	def write_four_role_stage_marker(out_root: Path) -> Path:
521	    """Persist the stage marker (P2) under `out_root`; return the written path.
522	
523	    Idempotent file write (no network, no spend). Called from the Gate-B production entrypoint so
524	    every benchmark run records its stage. Skipped in offline tests that inject a fake transport
525	    and call the seam directly (this lives on the live `run_gate_b_query` path).
526	    """

531	        encoding="utf-8",

533	    return marker_path

536	# I-cap-005 (#1068): the FULL-CAPABILITY benchmark env slate, applied by Gate-B itself so a run is at
537	# full depth REGARDLESS of the operator's shell env (the prior throttle was caused by setting the DEAD
538	# `PG_LIVE_*` names instead of the real `PG_SWEEP_*` knobs, + STORM/tracker never enabled). Every value
539	# is `setdefault` so an explicit operator override still wins (LAW VI). The slate is applied BEFORE the
540	# sweep is imported so import-time module constants (caps/timeouts) also see the full values.
541	_FULL_CAPABILITY_BENCHMARK_SLATE: dict[str, str] = {
542	    # I-fetch-002 (#1168): the WHOLE run fetches ~1000 sites TOTAL per question, NOT 1000 + additive
543	    # lanes. The operator's ~1000 budget is SPLIT across the four real fetch lanes so they SUM to ~1000
544	    # (the prior 1000 here was the MAIN lane alone, on top of which agentic/deepener/R-6 each added more
545	    # — silently overshooting the budget). The four lanes, FLOOR-applied below (max(existing, slate)):
546	    #   PG_SWEEP_FETCH_CAP            740  main Serper/S2/OpenAlex lane (total URLs after dedup, /query)
547	    #   PG_AGENTIC_BENCHMARK_URL_CAP  100  agentic-discovery harvest (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:3162)
548	    #   PG_SWEEP_DEEPENER_URL_CAP      60  citation-snowball deepener (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:3038)
549	    #   PG_R6_EXPAND_FETCH_CAP         40  R-6 completeness re-expansion (run_honest_sweep_r3.py:2961)
550	    #   PG_STORM_URL_FETCH_CAP         60  STORM web-results seed lane (I-beatboth-fix-000 BB-006)
551	    #   --------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
552	    #   SUM                          1000  ≈ the ~1000-site/question budget (operator no-overshoot).
553	    #   I-beatboth-fix-000 (Codex P1): main lane trimmed 800->740 to ABSORB the new STORM seed lane so
554	    #   the total fetch stays at the hard 1000 envelope (no ~1060 overshoot).
555	    # NOTE: PG_STORM_MAX_BENCHMARK_QUERIES (30) and PG_MAX_SUBQUERIES (15) below are QUERY-BREADTH
556	    # counts (how many search queries are issued), NOT URLs — they are deliberately NOT part of the
557	    # ~1000-URL sum. .env has no override for any of these (checked I-fetch-002), so the floor lands.
558	    # Retrieval breadth — the REAL run_one_query knobs (PG_SWEEP_*, default 12/12/40). NOT PG_LIVE_*.
559	    "PG_SWEEP_FETCH_CAP": "740",   # MAIN lane: total URLs/query (lane 1/4); 800->740 to absorb STORM seed lane (1000 total)
560	    "PG_SWEEP_MAX_SERPER": "100",
561	    "PG_SWEEP_MAX_S2": "100",
562	    # FX-17 (#1126): Serper `num` is a PAGE size (max ~20); breadth needs the new PAGINATION budget.
563	    # Without these the benchmark stays single-page (~20/query) despite MAX_SERPER=100. 60 over <=3
564	    # pages = up to 3 Serper pages/query.
565	    "PG_SERPER_TOTAL_PER_QUERY": "60",
566	    "PG_SERPER_MAX_PAGES": "3",
567	    # I-deepfix-001 (#1344) PURITY BUILD: STORM is a LOSER — killed. STORM is the core loser the operator
568	    # saw fire (interview/query-expansion engine). Slate "0" + removed from FORCE_ON + REQUIRED + added to

content.text: Nothing unprofessional yet - lots of jargon but domain-legitimate. Let's keep reading forward.

2794	# FIX: each marker is now a ``_FiringMarker`` predicate — ``must_contain`` (the success-specific substring)
2795	# PLUS ``forbid`` (degraded / failure / premature substrings that, if present in the SAME log line, prove the
2796	# winner did NOT genuinely fire). A line counts as a GENUINE fire iff it contains ``must_contain`` AND NONE of
2797	# ``forbid``. ``conditional=True`` marks data-dependent winners (W4 mineru25 fires only if a clinical PDF was
2798	# fetched; W10 NLI merge only with >=2 cross-cluster keys) whose ABSENCE is allowed — the audit treats them as
2799	# conditional-present, never a fail-on-absent. Each ``must_contain`` is file:line-verified against a REAL

2800	# producer SUCCESS line (see the per-row producer ref) so the contract can never silently point at a phantom
2801	# string. The matcher ``firing_marker_matched`` is the single source of truth the post-run audit AND the
2802	# durable section-test both consume, so "tightened to success-specific" is enforced, not merely documented.
2803	#
2804	# W9 is DARK (no run-path consumer) and is intentionally ABSENT from this contract — it is handled by the
2805	# LOUD W9 operator-ack gate, never grep-asserted on the run.

2894	def firing_marker_matched(marker: _FiringMarker, log_text: str) -> bool:
2895	    """True iff ``log_text`` contains at least one line that is a GENUINE fire of ``marker``: a line that
2896	    contains ``marker.must_contain`` AND contains NONE of ``marker.forbid``. The per-line check (not a global
2897	    ``and not any(forbid in log_text)``) is deliberate: a degraded line and a genuine line can BOTH appear in
2898	    the same run (e.g. one section degrades, another genuinely composes) — the winner fired iff ANY line is a
2899	    clean genuine fire. This is the single source of truth the post-run §-1.1 audit and the durable section-
2900	    test both consume. Pure string logic — no spend, no network."""
2901	    must = marker.must_contain
2902	    forbid = marker.forbid
2903	    for line in log_text.splitlines():
2904	        if must in line and not any(bad in line for bad in forbid):
2905	            return True
2906	    return False
2907

2909	def firing_marker_contract_substrings() -> dict[str, str]:
2910	    """The {winner -> must_contain} view of the contract — the success-specific substring the post-run audit
2911	    greps for each winner. Back-compat surface for any consumer that only needs the positive substring; the
2912	    forbid/conditional discrimination lives in ``firing_marker_matched``."""
2913	    return {k: m.must_contain for k, m in _WINNER_FIRING_MARKER_CONTRACT.items()}

2928	def assert_cross_source_synthesis_fired(log_text: str) -> None:
2929	    """WS-2 fail-loud M6 firing assertion (post-run, pure string logic — no spend, no network).
2930	
2931	    When PG_CROSS_SOURCE_SYNTHESIS is ON (the slate force-pins it) AND the run's cross_source_synthesis
2932	    producer reported that anchored cross-source pair(s) EXISTED (>=2 same-anchor, distinct-cluster
2933	    baskets) yet 0 analytical units survived per-clause re-verify across the WHOLE run, the M6 analytical
2934	    layer is a SILENT NO-OP (flag-on but dark) — raise RuntimeError. This is the exact firing failure the
2935	    drb_72 re-smoke shipped: slate-on but ``cross_source_analytical_units == 0`` while eligible pairs
2936	    existed.
2937	
2938	    A run with NO anchored pairs (the conditional-absence case — no silent-no-op line was ever logged)
2939	    NEVER raises: analytical yield EMERGES from real anchored pairs, it is never forced (§-1.3). A run
2940	    that produced >=1 analytical unit anywhere (a "composed" line present) NEVER raises even if one
2941	    section was individually barren — ``cross_source_analytical_units > 0`` at the run level. Slate-OFF is
2942	    a no-op (the feature's own PG_CROSS_SOURCE_SYNTHESIS is the LAW-VI kill-switch that disarms BOTH the
2943	    feature and this assertion). Consumed by the post-run §-1.1 audit alongside the winner firing-marker
2944	    grep; the durable section-test exercises the same function."""
2945	    if os.getenv("PG_CROSS_SOURCE_SYNTHESIS", "0").strip().lower() not in ("1", "true", "yes", "on"):
2946	        return
2947	    lines = log_text.splitlines()
2948	    eligible_but_barren = any(_CROSS_SOURCE_SILENT_NOOP_MARKER in ln for ln in lines)
2949	    genuinely_fired = any(_CROSS_SOURCE_FIRED_MARKER in ln for ln in lines)
2950	    if eligible_but_barren and not genuinely_fired:
2951	        raise RuntimeError(
2952	            "benchmark post-run FAILED [WINNER-FIRES M6]: PG_CROSS_SOURCE_SYNTHESIS is ON and the run "
2953	            "logged anchored cross-source pair(s) with 0 analytical units surviving per-clause re-verify "
2954	            "for the WHOLE run — the M6 cross-source analytical layer is a SILENT NO-OP "
2955	            "(cross_source_analytical_units == 0 while >=2 same-anchor baskets existed). Investigate "
2956	            "cross_source_synthesis.license_relation / the per-clause re-verify; do NOT ship a run whose "
2957	            "analytical layer produced nothing while eligible pairs existed."
2958	        )

3012	class _CrossSourceMarkerCaptureHandler(logging.Handler):
3013	    """Append the ``cross_source_synthesis`` module logger's message lines into ``sink`` so the post-run M6
3014	    canary can assert the analytical layer fired. A logging handler must NEVER raise into the caller, so
3015	    ``emit`` swallows any formatting error. Sequential-sweep safe (§8.4 — one query at a time); attached +
3016	    detached around a single ``asyncio.run(run_gate_b_query(...))`` so it never leaks across queries."""
3017	
3018	    def __init__(self, sink: list[str]) -> None:
3019	        super().__init__()
3020	        self._sink = sink
3021	
3022	    def emit(self, record: logging.LogRecord) -> None:
3023	        try:
3024	            self._sink.append(record.getMessage())
3025	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a logging handler must never propagate an error to the caller
3026	            pass

3029	class _ActivationMarkerCaptureHandler(logging.Handler):
3030	    """Capture the Wave-3a ``[activation] <module>:`` marker lines so the post-run activation canary can
3031	    read the sink the markers actually reach. 10 of the 11 activated modules emit their marker via a Python
3032	    MODULE logger (``logger.info(...)``) that basicConfig streams to STDOUT ONLY — it NEVER reaches
3033	    run_dir/run_log.txt (Fable P0). The sweep runs run_gate_b_query IN-PROCESS, so attaching this to the
3034	    ROOT logger for the query's duration captures those records (child loggers propagate to root). Keeps
3035	    ONLY records whose message begins with ``[activation] `` (never the whole run's log stream — bounded
3036	    memory). A logging handler must NEVER raise into the caller, so ``emit`` swallows any formatting error.
3037	    Sequential-sweep safe (§8.4 — one query at a time); attached + detached around a single
3038	    ``asyncio.run(run_gate_b_query(...))`` so it never leaks across queries."""
3039	
3040	    def __init__(self, sink: list[str]) -> None:
3041	        super().__init__()
3042	        self._sink = sink
3043	
3044	    def emit(self, record: logging.LogRecord) -> None:
3045	        try:
3046	            _msg = record.getMessage()
3047	            if _msg.startswith(_ACTIVATION_MARKER_PREFIX):
3048	                self._sink.append(_msg)
3049	        except Exception:  # noqa: BLE001 — a logging handler must never propagate an error to the caller
3050	            pass

3053	def _run_m6_firing_canary(
3054	    log_lines: list[str],
3055	    status: str,
3056	    *,
3057	    smoke_scale: bool,
3058	    domain: str,
3059	    slug: str,
3060	) -> str:
3061	    """POST-RUN M6 firing canary (FIX 3). Mirrors the breadth-canary pattern: on a RELEASED, non-smoke run,
3062	    assert the M6 cross-source analytical layer fired (or was conditionally absent) from the captured
3063	    ``cross_source_synthesis`` marker lines. A GENUINE silent-no-op (anchored cross-source pair(s) existed
3064	    but 0 analytical units survived per-clause re-verify) raises RuntimeError -> "FAILED" (caller sets
3065	    overall_rc=1). ``assert_cross_source_synthesis_fired`` ALSO self-skips on PG_CROSS_SOURCE_SYNTHESIS off
3066	    (flag-off => early return, no raise). Reuses the breadth canary's released-status universe so the M6
3067	    canary applies exactly where a full-contract report was rendered. Returns a one-line sweep-record status.
3068	    Faithfulness-neutral: reads captured run telemetry, asserts nothing about any verdict."""
3069	    if status not in _BREADTH_CANARY_RELEASED_STATUSES:
3070	        return f"skip:status={status or '<none>'}"
3071	    if smoke_scale:
3072	        return "skip:smoke_scale"
3073	    log_text = "\n".join(log_lines)
3074	    try:
3075	        assert_cross_source_synthesis_fired(log_text)
3076	    except RuntimeError as _m6_exc:
3077	        logging.getLogger("run_gate_b").error(
3078	            "M6 cross-source firing canary FAILED for %s/%s: %s", domain, slug, _m6_exc,
3079	        )
3080	        print(f"<<< {domain} / {slug}: M6 cross-source firing canary FAILED: {_m6_exc}")
3081	        return "FAILED"
3082	    print(f"<<< {domain} / {slug}: M6 cross-source firing canary=ok")
3083	    return "ok"

3087	# Two DETECTORS that guard against the "false-fired pipeline": the winner slate is ON, the writer-path
3088	# logs are busy, yet the rendered report is still shallow/degraded. Each asserts a STRUCTURAL
3089	# contradiction (an ELIGIBLE-YET-ZERO condition), NEVER a word/citation/source COUNT threshold (§-1.3:
3090	# such counts are BANNED as quality signals). Both self-skip unless the opt-in PG_SHALLOW_REPORT_CANARY
3091	# flag is truthy (default OFF => byte-identical, the canary never runs). Faithfulness-neutral: they READ
3092	# post-run telemetry lines and raise for investigation; they touch NO verdict, threshold, judge, or gate.

3094	# Producer marker lines (stable literals) the two canaries parse:

3096	#       "[depth-synthesis] D8-thread: baskets_total=.. drafted=.. kept_findings=.. (cross=.. single=..)"

3099	_SHALLOW_REPORT_CANARY_ENV = "PG_SHALLOW_REPORT_CANARY"

3116	def assert_depth_synthesis_fired(log_text: str) -> None:
3117	    """Wave-1d shallow canary #1 (post-run, pure string logic — no spend, no network).
3118	
3119	    STRUCTURAL contradiction: the grounded DEPTH cross-source synthesis pass DRAFTED >=1 ELIGIBLE
3120	    high-corroboration basket (the pre-pass only drafts baskets clearing the DEFINITIONAL
3121	    ``>=2 distinct-origin members`` floor) YET the run kept ZERO synthesized findings
3122	    (``kept_findings==0``). That is the "0 analytical units when eligible pairs exist" failed
3123	    validation — the depth layer is a SILENT NO-OP (flag-on but dark). Raise RuntimeError.
3124	
3125	    NEVER a count target: a run that drafted 0 eligible baskets (``drafted==0`` — a single-source /
3126	    low-corroboration corpus) NEVER raises (§-1.3, depth is never FORCED); a run that kept >=1 finding
3127	    (``kept_findings>=1``, of ANY magnitude) NEVER raises. Only the zero-when-eligible contradiction
3128	    decides — no magnitude threshold. Reads the EXISTING depth-synthesis D8-thread telemetry line and
3129	    asserts nothing about any verdict (the frozen faithfulness engine is untouched). Self-skips when
3130	    PG_SHALLOW_REPORT_CANARY is off."""

3135	    # even when another section kept findings — that is still the intended eligible-yet-zero semantics
3136	    # (a per-line structural contradiction), documented here for future producer changes.

3141	            raise RuntimeError(

3145	                "eligible baskets existed. Investigate depth_synthesis.synthesize_cross_source_findings "
3146	                "/ the per-sentence re-ground; do NOT ship a report whose depth layer produced nothing "

3152	    """Wave-1d shallow canary #2 (post-run, pure string logic — no spend, no network).
3153

3155	    (``corroboration_count>=2`` — the SAME distinct-origin basis as the basket denominator, so a
3156	    same-host near-dup pair is never miscounted) YET ZERO consolidation baskets reached composition
3157	    with ``verified_support_origin_count>=2``. That is the finding_dedup->basket keystone silently NOT
3158	    producing multi-origin baskets — the documented "787 rows -> mostly-singleton baskets, Multi-source
3159	    corroborated: 0" regression (credibility_pass.py:51-56). Raise RuntimeError.
3160	
3161	    NEVER a count target: a run whose finding_dedup produced 0 multi-origin clusters (a single-source /
3162	    non-overlapping corpus) NEVER raises (§-1.3, corroboration is never FORCED); a run that produced
3163	    >=1 multi-origin basket (of ANY magnitude) NEVER raises. Absent the flag-gated telemetry line (the
3164	    Wave-1d flag was off during the run, or finding_dedup did not run) the canary has no data and NEVER
3165	    raises. Reads only telemetry; touches no verdict. Self-skips when PG_SHALLOW_REPORT_CANARY is off."""

3182	def _run_shallow_report_canary(
3183	    log_text: str,
3184	    status: str,
3185	    *,
3186	    smoke_scale: bool,
3187	    domain: str,
3188	    slug: str,
3189	) -> str:
3190	    """POST-RUN shallow-report canary wrapper (Wave-1d). Mirrors ``_run_m6_firing_canary``: on a
3191	    RELEASED, non-smoke run, run BOTH structural detectors over the run_log text. A GENUINE
3192	    eligible-yet-zero contradiction raises RuntimeError -> "FAILED" (caller sets overall_rc=1). Both
3193	    asserts self-skip when PG_SHALLOW_REPORT_CANARY is off (wrapper returns "skip:disabled" first).
3194	    Reuses the breadth/M6 released-status universe so it applies exactly where a full-contract report
3195	    was rendered. Faithfulness-neutral: reads run telemetry, asserts nothing about any verdict.
3196	    Returns a one-line sweep-record status."""
3197	    if not _shallow_report_canary_enabled():
3198	        return "skip:disabled"
3199	    if status not in _BREADTH_CANARY_RELEASED_STATUSES:
3200	        return f"skip:status={status or '<none>'}"
3201	    if smoke_scale:
3202	        return "skip:smoke_scale"
3203	    try:
3204	        assert_depth_synthesis_fired(log_text)
3205	        assert_multi_origin_baskets_exist(log_text)
3206	    except RuntimeError as _sc_exc:
3207	        logging.getLogger("run_gate_b").error(
3208	            "shallow-report canary FAILED for %s/%s: %s", domain, slug, _sc_exc,
3209	        )
3210	        print(f"<<< {domain} / {slug}: shallow-report canary FAILED: {_sc_exc}")
3211	        return "FAILED"
3212	    print(f"<<< {domain} / {slug}: shallow-report canary=ok")
3213	    return "ok"

3216	# ── I-deepfix-001 (#1344) Wave-3a — FAIL-LOUD ACTIVATION CANARY ───────────────────────────────────────
3217	# The wall that guarantees, on a RELEASED paid run, that every ACTIVATED new-core module ACTUALLY FIRED
3218	# and no OLD/WRONG module silently replaced it (the exact failure the operator has hit repeatedly).
3219	#
3220	# MARKER TRANSPORT (Fable P0 fix — the canary MUST read the sink the markers actually reach): 10 of the
3221	# 11 modules emit their ``[activation] <module>:`` line via a Python MODULE logger (``logger.info(...)``)
3222	# which basicConfig streams to STDOUT ONLY — it never reaches ``run_dir/run_log.txt`` (only the ``_log``
3223	# tee writes that file; run_honest_sweep_r3.py:57-61 basicConfig has NO FileHandler, :9075-9078 ``_log``).
3224	# So the canary CANNOT read those markers from run_log.txt (an earlier version did — it would have
3225	# FALSE-FAILED every healthy run and been BLIND on the old-path tripwires). Instead — mirroring the M6
3226	# ``_CrossSourceMarkerCaptureHandler`` — an ``_ActivationMarkerCaptureHandler`` is attached to the ROOT
3227	# logger for the duration of each in-process query (the sweep runs run_gate_b_query IN-PROCESS, so the
3228	# module-logger records ARE visible) and captures every ``[activation] `` record into a per-query buffer.
3229	# The canary asserts over that buffer TEXT COMBINED WITH run_log.txt: the buffer carries the 10 module-
3230	# logger markers (9 positives + the anchor-equality degrade tripwire), and run_log.txt carries the one
3231	# ``_log``-emitted marker (provenance_reanchor, run_honest_sweep_r3.py:14876). For each module whose flag
3232	# is ON at CALL time, the canary asserts THREE STRUCTURAL facts:
3233	#   (a) the module's POSITIVE marker is PRESENT  (the module RAN);
3234	#   (b) the module's honesty booleans are HEALTHY (degraded/noop/wall_truncated False, build_ok/
3235	#       input_threaded True — the marker did-not-degrade to a legacy fallback);
3236	#   (c) the module's OLD/degrade signal is ABSENT/ZERO (the anchor-equality pairing line absent; the
3237	#       provenance-reanchor ``local_window`` count == 0 — the forbidden local-window fallback leg recovered
3238	#       nothing, Fable P0 option-B: the ``reanchored_local_window:`` soft-warning is NEVER logged, so the
3239	#       inverted tripwire is routed through a telemetry COUNTER surfaced on the provenance marker instead).
3240	# ANY of {positive marker absent, honesty bool bad, old/degrade signal present} => RuntimeError => the
3241	# wrapper returns "FAILED" => the caller sets overall_rc=1 (fail loud).
3242	#
3243	# §-1.3 STRUCTURAL RULE (explicit): this is ELIGIBLE-YET-WRONG detection, NEVER a quality count threshold.
3244	# The ONLY count-like checks permitted are (i) the PRESENCE/ABSENCE of a marker line, (ii) the value of a
3245	# BOOLEAN field, and (iii) an inverted degrade counter that must be EXACTLY ZERO (``local_window==0`` — the
3246	# forbidden path fired zero times, equivalent to "old marker absent"; NOT a ">=K quality target"). A
3247	# positive marker PRESENT with count=0 (directional_merges=0, pairs=0, facets=0) is ALLOWED — an
3248	# eligible-yet-zero contradiction is a SEPARATE shallow-canary concern, not this canary's.
3249	#
3250	# The canary SELF-SCOPES to the activated slate: a module whose flag is OFF at call time is never asserted
3251	# (no marker demanded). Each module's flag ON/OFF is decided with the SAME truthy predicate its producer
3252	# uses (Fable P2: expert-facet / sub-entity accept ONLY {"1","true"}; provenance/span accept
3253	# {"1","true","yes","on","enabled"}; the rest use the not-in-off-tokens blocklist) so a slate value like
3254	# "yes"/"on" can NEVER make the canary demand a marker a strict-whitelist producer would not emit. Both the
3255	# opt-in kill-switch and the whole block mirror the Wave-1d shallow-canary OFF-purity contract EXACTLY:
3256	# default-OFF ``PG_ACTIVATION_CANARY`` => byte-identical (the canary never runs, NO capture handler is
3257	# attached, AND the sweep record gains NO new key); a missing/unreadable run_log records ``skip:no-run-log``
3258	# (a fail-loud detector must never false-green on no data). Faithfulness-neutral: reads run telemetry only.
3259	_ACTIVATION_CANARY_ENV = "PG_ACTIVATION_CANARY"

3262	# flag is "ON" iff its env value, lower-cased/stripped, is NOT one of these — so a slate's force-EXACT "1"
3263	# reads ON. Whitelist producers (expert-facet / sub-entity / provenance) carry their own ``flag_whitelist``.
3264	_ACTIVATION_FLAG_OFF_TOKENS = ("", "0", "false", "no", "off")

3682	        # fail/degrade branch on the armed emit => NO unavailable_failopen twin. Producer
3683	        # _wall_classify_rescue_enabled accepts 1/true/on/yes.
3684	        name="wall_classify_rescue",
3685	        env_flag="PG_WALL_CLASSIFY_RESCUE",
3686	        positive_re=re.compile(
3687	            r"\[activation\] wall_classify_rescue: armed enrich_parallel=(?:True|False)"
3688	        ),
3689	        bool_checks=(),
3690	        absent_markers=(),
3691	        flag_whitelist=("1", "true", "on", "yes"),

3695	# I-deepfix-001 (#1369) drb_72 FIX WAVE — LOG-READ-PROVEN flags (NOT hard-registered above). Each of the 6
3696	# fixwave flags is force-ON / value-pinned on the slate (so the fix is ACTIVE), but its realized-effect
3697	# [activation] marker fires on a CONDITIONAL seam, so a run-level hard _ActivationMarkerSpec would false-
3698	# CRASH a legitimate released report that skips the seam (the exact anti-pattern the Wave-9 deferral note
3699	# and the conservatism mandate forbid). They are proven on run-day by GREPPING the marker in the run log,
3700	# NOT by the crashing canary. Run-day PROVEN-table greps (marker literal -> producer):
3701	#   PG_SUMMARY_TABLE_SOURCE_CONSOLIDATE  "[activation] summary_table_source_consolidate: clusters="   (summary_table.py; only when the question requests a titled column-header table)
3702	#   PG_CWF_PROMOTION_TOPICAL_GATE        "[activation] promotion_topical_gate: demoted="              (weighted_enrichment.py; only when the M5 promotion-eligibility partition runs)
3703	#   PG_QUANTIFIED_PRUNE_UNREFERENCED_SOURCED "[activation] quantified_prune_unreferenced_sourced: pruned=" (tradeoff_modeler.py; only when the QUANTIFIED stage runs)
3704	#   PG_CONTRACT_FALSE_GAP_KSPAN          "[activation] contract_false_gap_kspan: slot="               (contract_section_runner.py; only per gap-candidate slot — a clean report emits nothing)
3705	#   PG_UNCOVERED_FACT_SUBJECT_GATE       "[activation] uncovered_fact_subject_gate: withheld="        (verified_compose.py; only on a junk withhold — a clean report emits nothing)
3706	#   PG_MIN_FETCH_YIELD / fetch_yield_gate "[activation] fetch_yield_gate: rate="                      (live_retriever.py; ONLY when the FETCH runs — EXPECTED-present on the box2 fresh run, EXPECTED-ABSENT on the box1 --resume)
3707	#   PG_CWF_ISSN_CHROME                   "[activation] issn_masthead_chrome: fired="                  (weighted_enrichment.py; ONLY when an ISSN masthead-recital chrome line is matched — a clean report emits nothing)
3708	#   PG_CWF_CHART_ALT_CHROME              "[activation] chart_alt_chrome: fired="                      (weighted_enrichment.py; ONLY when a chart alt-text enumeration line is matched — a clean report emits nothing)
3709	#   PG_CWF_EMPTY_BULLET_DROP             "[activation] empty_bullet_drop: dropped="                   (weighted_enrichment.py; ONLY when an empty / marker-only bullet is dropped — a clean report emits nothing)

3712	def _activation_canary_enabled() -> bool:
3713	    """``PG_ACTIVATION_CANARY`` opt-in kill-switch (default OFF). Read at CALL time (LAW VI). OFF =>
3714	    ``assert_activation_markers_fired`` early-returns AND the post-run wrapper is never invoked AND the
3715	    sweep record gains NO ``activation_canary`` key => byte-identical to pre-Wave-3a. Mirrors
3716	    ``_shallow_report_canary_enabled`` exactly."""
3717	    return os.getenv(_ACTIVATION_CANARY_ENV, "0").strip().lower() in ("1", "true", "yes", "on")

content.text: Continuing to be extremely clean, professionally documented (albeit incredibly verbose). Let's keep going through the remaining unread stretches.

content.content:
3720	def _activation_flag_on(env_flag: str, whitelist: tuple | None = None, default_on: bool = False) -> bool:
3721	    """True iff ``env_flag`` reads ON, decided with the SAME truthy predicate the module's producer uses
3722	    (Fable P2). ``whitelist`` not None => ON iff the lower-cased value is in it (strict-whitelist producer);
3723	    else the not-in-off-tokens BLOCKLIST default. ``default_on`` (Wave-6b) => an UNSET flag reads ON, matching
3724	    a DEFAULT-ON blocklist producer (e.g. summary_table_enabled() defaults to "1"); an explicit "0"/off still
3725	    reads OFF. Read at CALL time (LAW VI). All-OFF-default flags are force-EXACT "1" on a released slate, so
3726	    both predicates read "1" as ON; the whitelist only prevents a slate value like "yes"/"on" from
3727	    over-demanding a marker a strict-whitelist producer never emits."""
3728	    _val = os.getenv(env_flag, "1" if default_on else "0").strip().lower()
3729	    if whitelist is not None:
3730	        return _val in whitelist
3731	    return _val not in _ACTIVATION_FLAG_OFF_TOKENS
3732	
3733	
3734	def assert_activation_markers_fired(log_text: str) -> None:
3735	    """Wave-3a fail-loud ACTIVATION canary (post-run, pure string logic — no spend, no network).
3736	
3737	    For EACH activated module (its flag ON at CALL time), assert over the captured marker ``log_text`` that
3738	    its POSITIVE ``[activation]`` marker is PRESENT, its honesty booleans are HEALTHY, its inverted degrade
3739	    counter is EXACTLY ZERO, and its OLD/degrade marker is ABSENT. Any failure raises ``RuntimeError`` (the
3740	    wrapper maps it to "FAILED" => overall_rc=1).
3741	
3742	    STRUCTURAL presence/boolean/exact-zero ONLY, NEVER a quality count threshold (§-1.3): a positive marker
3743	    present with count=0 is ALLOWED (eligible-yet-zero is the shallow canary's concern). Self-scopes to the
3744	    ACTIVATED slate — a module whose flag is OFF is never asserted. Reads run telemetry only; asserts nothing
3745	    about any verdict (the frozen faithfulness engine is untouched). Self-skips when ``PG_ACTIVATION_CANARY``
3746	    is off. ``log_text`` is the module-logger capture buffer COMBINED with run_log.txt (see the sweep loop)."""
3747	    if not _activation_canary_enabled():
3748	        return
3749	    text = log_text or ""
3750	    for spec in (*_ACTIVATION_MARKER_SPECS, *_ACTIVATION_MARKER_SPECS_WAVE3):
3751	        if spec.flag_int_min is not None:
3752	            # NUMERIC infra flag (e.g. PG_QGEN_PARALLEL_QUERIES): ON iff int(value) >= threshold, so the
3753	            # serial default (1) never false-demands the >1-only marker (I-deepfix-001 Wave-3).
3754	            try:
3755	                _flag_on = int((os.getenv(spec.env_flag, "0") or "0").strip()) >= spec.flag_int_min
3756	            except ValueError:
3757	                _flag_on = False
3758	        else:
3759	            _flag_on = _activation_flag_on(spec.env_flag, spec.flag_whitelist, spec.flag_default_on)
3760	        if not _flag_on:
3761	            continue  # module not in the active slate => demand no marker (self-scoping)
3762	        # (a) POSITIVE marker present + (b) honesty booleans healthy + inverted degrade counter zero. Use
3763	        # finditer so a DEGRADE in ANY of several emissions (e.g. per-section) fails, not only the first.
3764	        if spec.positive_re is not None:
3765	            _matches = list(spec.positive_re.finditer(text))
3766	            if not _matches:
3767	                raise RuntimeError(
3768	                    f"activation canary FAILED [{spec.name.upper()} MARKER ABSENT]: flag {spec.env_flag} "
3769	                    f"is ON yet the captured markers carry NO positive '[activation] {spec.name}:' line — "
3770	                    f"the module did NOT fire on a released run (an old/wrong module may have silently "
3771	                    f"replaced it). Investigate the {spec.name} wiring; do NOT ship a report whose activated "
3772	                    f"module left no fire marker."
3773	                )
3774	            for _m in _matches:
3775	                for _field, _healthy in spec.bool_checks:
3776	                    _val = _m.group(_field)
3777	                    if _val != _healthy:
3778	                        raise RuntimeError(
3779	                            f"activation canary FAILED [{spec.name.upper()} DEGRADED]: marker field "
3780	                            f"{_field}={_val} (healthy={_healthy}) — the {spec.name} module fired but "
3781	                            f"DEGRADED to a legacy/fallback path on a released run. Investigate the "
3782	                            f"{spec.name} degrade cause; do NOT ship a report built by the degraded path."
3783	                        )
3784	                for _field, _exact in spec.exact_fields:
3785	                    _val = _m.group(_field)
3786	                    if _val != _exact:
3787	                        raise RuntimeError(
3788	                            f"activation canary FAILED [{spec.name.upper()} OLD/DEGRADE LEG FIRED]: marker "
3789	                            f"field {_field}={_val} (must be exactly {_exact}) — the FORBIDDEN legacy leg "
3790	                            f"recovered {_val} time(s) on a released run (it is pinned OFF on gate-B, so a "
3791	                            f"non-zero count means a regression re-opened it). Investigate {spec.name}; do "
3792	                            f"NOT ship a report the old leg helped build."
3793	                        )
3794	        # (c) OLD/degrade markers ABSENT (the old-path-fired tripwire).
3795	        for _degrade_literal in spec.absent_markers:
3796	            if _degrade_literal in text:
3797	                raise RuntimeError(
3798	                    f"activation canary FAILED [{spec.name.upper()} OLD/DEGRADE MARKER PRESENT]: the "
3799	                    f"captured markers contain '{_degrade_literal}' while flag {spec.env_flag} is ON — the "
3800	                    f"OLD/wrong path fired instead of (or alongside) the activated module. Investigate the "
3801	                    f"{spec.name} routing; do NOT ship a report the old path helped build."
3802	                )
3803

3805	def _run_activation_canary(
3806	    log_text: str,
3807	    status: str,
3808	    *,
3809	    smoke_scale: bool,
3810	    domain: str,
3811	    slug: str,
3812	) -> str:
3813	    """POST-RUN activation canary wrapper (Wave-3a). Mirrors ``_run_shallow_report_canary``: on a RELEASED,
3814	    non-smoke run, run the structural activation detector over the run_log text. A GENUINE activation
3815	    failure (marker absent / honesty bool bad / old-path marker present) raises ``RuntimeError`` -> "FAILED"
3816	    (caller sets overall_rc=1). Self-skips when ``PG_ACTIVATION_CANARY`` is off (returns "skip:disabled"
3817	    first). Reuses the breadth/M6/shallow released-status universe so it applies exactly where a
3818	    full-contract report was rendered. Faithfulness-neutral: reads run telemetry, asserts nothing about any
3819	    verdict. Returns a one-line sweep-record status."""
3820	    if not _activation_canary_enabled():
3821	        return "skip:disabled"
3822	    if status not in _BREADTH_CANARY_RELEASED_STATUSES:
3823	        return f"skip:status={status or '<none>'}"
3824	    if smoke_scale:
3825	        return "skip:smoke_scale"
3826	    try:
3827	        assert_activation_markers_fired(log_text)
3828	    except RuntimeError as _ac_exc:
3829	        logging.getLogger("run_gate_b").error(
3830	            "activation canary FAILED for %s/%s: %s", domain, slug, _ac_exc,
3831	        )
3832	        print(f"<<< {domain} / {slug}: activation canary FAILED: {_ac_exc}")
3833	        return "FAILED"
3834	    print(f"<<< {domain} / {slug}: activation canary=ok")
3835	    return "ok"

    # -- I-deepfix-001 (#1344) WAVE-1 ACTIVATION - T3 backend + four HARDEN-PIN default-ON flags --
    # Conscious 'winner or infra?' decision - allowlisted deliberately so the clean slate PASSES SLATE-
    # PURITY. §-1.3 weight-and-consolidate; FAITHFULNESS-NEUTRAL (frozen faithfulness engine untouched).
    "PG_WORKFORCE_T3_TARGETING",             # statistical-agency backend + T3 tier floor (WEIGHT, not drop)

    "PG_OPENALEX_DATE_FILTER",               # additive date-scoped OpenAlex lane (UNION on top of base, never drops)
    "PG_LANDMARK_EXPANDER",                  # in-window landmark-study query expander (adds on-topic queries)
    "PG_STANCE_DIVERSIFY_SEEDS",             # GENERAL stance/view-diversification seed lane (adds multi-viewpoint queries)
    # -- I-deepfix-001 (#1344) WAVE-4 CONTAMINATION (winner-or-infra: FAITHFULNESS-NEUTRAL contamination kill) --
    # The two BOOLEAN contamination-kill flags - conscious 'winner or infra?' decision, allowlisted

    "PG_OPENALEX_MATCH_VALIDATE",            # validate OpenAlex title-search match (withhold wrong metadata)
    "PG_RESOLVE_PUBDATE_FROM_HTML",          # activate the dark publication-date resolver (fetched-content date)
